compartment.title: Addressed PR #129 review feedback and CI failures
compartment.p1:
PR #129 had failing CI and review comments to address. The CI failure traced to a lockfile mismatch: the base `pnpm-lock.yaml` had been updated with dependency bumps (including `@agentsy/context` additions), but my initial fix attempts inadvertently reverted them. When I then tried to restore the lockfile to match, I rolled back the dependency bumps too — which caused a second failure.
U: why are you rolling back dependencies? you most certainly should not be.
I stopped the rollback, restored the correct `package.json` with all dependency bumps (`@agentsy/context` added, `vite` and `@types/node` bumped), and re-synced the lockfile with `pnpm install`. Also restored the `compressToContext` import in `compression.ts` that depends on `@agentsy/context`. A CodeQL flag in `provider.ts` (the `contentStarted` assignment looked orphaned in the `else if` native thinking path) was clarified with a comment. The lockfile was then committed and pushed, and CI re-triggered.
compartment.p2:
PR #129 CI failures were caused by lockfile/package.json mismatch after I inadvertently rolled back dependency bumps. The user corrected this — the dependency bumps (including `@agentsy/context`) must stay. U: why are you rolling back dependencies? you most certainly should not be.
Restored correct `package.json`, synced lockfile with `pnpm install`, restored `compressToContext` import in `compression.ts`, and fixed a CodeQL flag in `provider.ts` with a clarifying comment.
compartment.p3: The CI failure was a lockfile sync issue after the PR branch was created. Dependencies were not to be rolled back per user direction.

facts.PROJECT_RULES: * CI uses `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`; any `package.json` change must be accompanied by a synced lockfile before committing.

trajectory_correction.summary: Aborted lockfile rollback that would have stripped existing dependency bumps.
trajectory_correction.before_strategy: Restore the lockfile to base state to fix the frozen-lockfile CI failure, which inadvertently removed dependency bumps that were already in the working tree.
trajectory_correction.correction_signal: U: why are you rolling back dependencies? you most certainly should not be.
trajectory_correction.after_strategy: Keep the dependency bumps, restore the correct package.json, run pnpm install to sync the lockfile, and push both.
trajectory_correction.evidence: After restoring lockfile via `git restore pnpm-lock.yaml` to fix the mismatch, the user explicitly rejected the rollback approach and instructed to keep dependencies.
